cl-q001-c41ea3191cab92907d83.reviewer: OpenAI GPT-5.6 Thinking (AI-assisted manual citation audit)
cl-q001-c41ea3191cab92907d83.review_notes: The generated claim's attention-only replacement of recurrent/convolutional models, together with its cited global-dependency evidence, substantively covers the required claim. Treat as a valid merged/paraphrased match rather than a lexical false negative.

cl-q002-3cab1a55474dcd47a64a.reviewer: OpenAI GPT-5.6 Thinking (AI-assisted manual citation audit)
cl-q002-3cab1a55474dcd47a64a.review_notes: The generated statement that the Transformer is based solely on attention is a direct semantic match for relying on attention to draw dependencies; the lower token overlap is a matcher false negative.

cl-q004-ba317f113c67f72f7260.reviewer: OpenAI GPT-5.6 Thinking (AI-assisted manual citation audit)
cl-q004-ba317f113c67f72f7260.review_notes: The generated claim covers training on eight GPUs but omits the Adam schedule and warmup. It is not sufficient for formal full coverage of the conjunctive required claim.

cl-q015-281a83daa0567f6ae7ac.reviewer: OpenAI GPT-5.6 Thinking (AI-assisted manual citation audit)
cl-q015-281a83daa0567f6ae7ac.review_notes: A claim about leaving architectural ablations to future work does not express that the paper is primarily a survey/exploration rather than a new-method proposal. The lexical candidate is a false positive.

limitations: The diagnostic rate does not replace the historical formal Dev v2 metric.
It does not show that prompt v2 solved silent claim omission.
It cannot be extrapolated to Full-50 or production.
